summary: Successfully demonstrated arbitrary code execution through the /api/v1/custom_component endpoint. The exploit submits a malicious Python component that uses subprocess to execute system commands and writes a proof file, confirming complete control over the server.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should execute arbitrary Python code on the Langflow server by submitting a malicious custom component. The component will execute a shell command that writes a proof file to /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt containing the marker 'EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS_<username>'. This demonstrates the ability to execute arbitrary system commands with the privileges of the Langflow process.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully created the proof file at /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt containing the EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS marker followed by the username running the Langflow process. This confirms that the attacker-controlled Python code was executed on the server with full access to system resources.
verification_method: The PoC verification checks for the existence of /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt and validates it contains the expected EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS marker. The presence of this file proves that the malicious component code was executed, as only the exploited vulnerability could have created it with that specific content.
evidence_produced_by_run: Proof file /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt created on the target system
File contains the EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS marker followed by the current username
Custom component API returned HTTP 200, confirming code was processed
No errors or exceptions during the attack flow

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
